title: Intelligence Efficiency, Ben Geist | Compile 26
summary: Ben Geist argues that token spending shows diminishing returns in cost-performance and that improving context quality is more efficient than increasing compute. He frames language models as entropy reduction machines where context and sparse attention matter more than raw model size.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Ben Geist makes the case that spending more tokens yields diminishing returns on cost-performance, and that raising context quality beats adding compute. He frames language models as entropy reduction machines, arguing that context and sparse attention matter more than raw model size. The discussion touches test-time compute, multimodal learning, and multi-agent systems as part of the efficiency argument.
